description: Headline gated decision: a human principal (chargeback/fraud-ops lead) authorizes contesting a dispute with a SPECIFIC evidence package BEFORE the recipe fires Stripe's irreversible POST /v1/disputes/{id} (submit=true). Stripe allows one submission, no edits — so a signed record of WHO authorized WHAT evidence WHEN, before the terminal call, is the whole value. Analyst = performer (submits the package as the completion); lead = principal (accept/reject via /verdict). The engine enforces conditional-required evidence by category at structural validation — a package missing the category's required fields gets structuralValidation:INVALID and does not advance. evidencePackageHash binds the exact bytes: criteria carries sha256 of the package submitted to Stripe, the completion echoes it, an auditor recomputes over the actually-submitted evidence. GATE + SoD ARE ORCHESTRATOR-ENFORCED, NOT TYPE-ENFORCED (see README Lessons): gateMode:auto can override defaultGateMode:principal and bypass the human (F-791/api#838), and principal==performer self-commitment is not rejected (F-758/api#759). The orchestrator MUST route every fraud/high-value dispute through the principal gate, MUST NOT set gateMode:auto on this type, and MUST provision distinct analyst + lead identities.

disputeId.description: Stripe Dispute id (du_...; older docs show dp_) this decision authorizes a response to.

disputeRef.description: recordId of the chargeback-dispute-filed-v1 this decision descends from (chain lineage).

amount.description: Disputed amount in major units. The merchant's review-threshold input: amount >= threshold forces the human gate.

reason.description: Stripe raw reason. reason=fraudulent forces the human gate regardless of amount.

disputeCategory.description: Evidence-guidance category; must equal the completion evidence package's `category`. (Named disputeCategory in the criteria — `category` is a reserved record-metadata field name.)

evidenceDueBy.description: Carried from the filing so a decision rendered after the deadline is observable on-chain.

evidencePackageHash.description: sha256:<hex> of the exact, canonicalized Stripe evidence object the recipe will submit. Binds the authorization to specific bytes.

filesContentHash.description: Optional sha256:<hex> over the concatenated/manifested attached file contents (purpose=dispute_evidence uploads).

category.description: Category the assembled package addresses; must equal the record's criteria.category.

deliveryType.description: For product_not_received: whether the product was physically shipped or digitally delivered; selects which proof set is required.

evidencePackageHash.description: Must equal the record's criteria.evidencePackageHash — the bytes the principal is authorizing.

productDescription.description: Stripe evidence.product_description — what the customer purchased.

customerSignature.description: File id (file_...) of proof-of-delivery signature, purpose=dispute_evidence.

accessActivityLog.description: Stripe evidence.access_activity_log — server/CDN access records proving digital delivery.

duplicateChargeDocumentation.description: File id of documentation distinguishing this charge from the alleged duplicate.

refundPolicyDisclosure.description: How/when the refund policy was disclosed to the customer.

uncategorizedText.description: Stripe evidence.uncategorized_text — free narrative. In test mode the strings winning_evidence / losing_evidence force the outcome.
